sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Bar is blue. Let me click one cell to see what color it becomes.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 30}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
grid = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    r_center = sum(rows) / len(rows)
    c_center = sum(cols) / len(cols)
    if 47 <= r_center <= 63 and 22 <= c_center <= 38 and n['pixels'] <= 10:
        r = int(round((r_center - 50) / 5))
        c = int(round((c_center - 25) / 5))
        grid[(r, c)] = n['color']

print("Grid after clicking top cell (bar is blue):")
for r in range(3):
    row_str = ""
    for c in range(3):
        row_str += grid.get((r, c), '?') + " "
    print(f"  Row {r}: {row_str}")

# Also check the bar color
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b' or n['color'] == 'S':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Blue/Sky node: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 30}])
